test_cases.input: URL: https://ministry-of-health.example.gov/press-releases/pandemic-response-update-may-2026. Context: Government press release with case fatality rate statistics contradicting earlier statements. Minister's office has a history of quietly revising press releases. Filing story Thursday. No legal evidence needed — editorial preservation only.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Strongest craft: step 8's advice to screenshot the last-modified date stamp specifically, and step 9's re-archiving timeline — practical judgment, not template output

test_cases.input: Two URLs: (1) CEO tweet with forward-looking revenue claim, (2) corporate earnings call transcript contradicting the tweet. Potential securities fraud case. Both need legal evidence preservation. Tweet could be deleted at any moment.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Strongest craft: priority reordering — archiving the tweet first because it is the deletion risk; the note about capturing deletion time itself if the tweet disappears mid-process

test_cases.input: URL: Municipal planning portal with JavaScript-rendered interactive map for controversial housing development environmental impact assessment. Developer has political connections to council. Preserve before publication. Not legal evidence now, but want to keep option open.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Strongest craft: framing 'keeping the legal option open' as a five-minute insurance policy rather than a formal section; advice to re-archive after publication to create comparison record
